rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Protective Order Of Confidential Information And Motion To Quash
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 27, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service for the Petition on file. Proof of Service filed on May 25, 2023 does not include a completed Notice and Acknowledgement of Receipt signed by the respondent. CCP 415.30. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief Under Ccp 473(B) And Request For Permission To File Tardy Oppositions To: (1) Cross-Defendants Sotheby'S International Realty, Inc. And Mary Lou Castellanos' Motion To Strike, And (2) Demurrer To 3rd Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 27, 2023 line 4. CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S MOTION FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP 473(B) AND REQUEST FOR PERMISSION TO FILE TARDY OPPOSITIONS TO: 1) CROSS-DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE AND 2) DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on October 27, 2023 Cross-Complainant to file a declaration attaching copies of the proposed oppositions to the Demurrer and Motion to Strike. If Cross-Complainant fails to comply, the demurrer and the motion to strike will be granted without leave to amend. If Cross-Complainant timely complies (as instructed by September 29, 2023 minute order), the hearings on the demurrer and the motion to strike will be continued to November 9, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CHL INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHITO KUMAR SAHA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Disqualify And Show Causes
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 27, 2023 line 5. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO DISQUALIFY AND SHOW CAUSES is DENIED. Moving party fails to show any basis for disqualification. Saha's counsel was never retained by Punj; Punj never considered retaining this counsel; Punj never consulted this counsel independent from Saha; Punj never provided any information to Saha's counsel and Saha that would not have been otherwise provided to the landlord regarding the lease. Saha's request for OSC made in opposition to the motion is not considered and should be raised, if necessary and proper, as a separate matter. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHL INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHITO KUMAR SAHA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Cross-Defendant Vikrant Punj And His Counsel And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 27, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL RETURN AND DESTRUCTION OF PRIVLEDGED COMMUNICATIONS is GRANTED in part, DENIED in part and continued in part. The motion is DENIED as to communications between Christine Tour-Sarkissian and Gregory Nerland. The motion is granted as to the e-mail(s) from Tour-Sarkissian forwarding communications with Nerland to Saha. Motion is otherwise continued to November 3, 2023 for the moving party to file a serve a code compliant privilege log no later than October 30, 2023 by 3:00 p.m. "The information in the privilege log must be sufficiently specific to allow a determination of whether each withheld document is or is not [in] fact privileged." [Wellpoint Health Networks, Inc. v. Sup.Ct. (McCombs) (1997) 59 CA4th 110, 130, 68 CR2d 844, 857; see Catalina Island Yacht Club v. Sup.Ct. (Beatty) (2015) 242 CA4th 1116, 1130, 195 CR3d 694, 704 & fn. 5-privilege log deficient due to failure to describe documents or contents (other than noting they were emails with counsel) since not all communications with attorneys are privileged. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MIHAL EMBERTON VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY GOVERMENT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 27, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, with leave to amend. TAC fails to state a claim because Plaintiff has not exhausted her administrative remedies to show the issues are ripe. Plaintiff must explain how she exhausted her administrative remedies. Request for judicial notice of Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint is granted. (Evid. rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC VS. CHRISTOPHER MIKA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 27, 2023 line 13. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer on basis of nonpayment of rent. Defendant's allegations that (1) Plaintiff is not exempt from S.F. Admin. Code Section 37.2(r)(4) and (2) Plaintiff served two notices but only based this action on one of them, are factual matters not appropriate for demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
